name: ExtensionRatioRange
title: R5: Range of ratio values (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `RatioRange` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`RatioRange` 0..* `RatioRange`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `RatioRange` 0..* `RatioRange`

Following are the generation technical comments:
FHIR R5 ComplexType `RatioRange` is representable via extensions in FHIR R4.
Element `RatioRange` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `RatioRange` is not mapped.
Element `RatioRange` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
FHIR R5 ComplexType `RatioRange` is representable via extensions in FHIR R4.
Element `RatioRange` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `RatioRange` is not mapped.
Element `RatioRange` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The stated low and high value are assumed to have arbitrarily high precision when it comes to determining which values are in the range. I.e. 1.99 is not in the range 2 -> 3.

element.short: DataType slice for a FHIR R5 `RatioRange` value
element.definition: Slice to indicate the presence of a R5 `RatioRange` in FHIR R4

element.comment:
Element `RatioRange.lowNumerator` is part of an existing definition because parent element `RatioRange` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `RatioRange.lowNumerator` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `RatioRange` is not mapped.
Element `RatioRange.lowNumerator` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If the low element is missing, the low boundary is not known.
